title: Chapter 2 - Requirements Check
description: Verify you understand the basic tools needed to create HTML files.

questions.prompt: Which tool is best for writing HTML code?

options.label: A plain text editor like Notepad
A code editor like VS Code

explanations.overview: You need a text editor to create and edit HTML files.

byOptionId.vscode: Correct — a code editor like VS Code is recommended for writing HTML.
byOptionId.notepad: Incorrect — a plain text editor like Notepad works only for basic HTML editing. When you start working on larger projects, you will get confused by missing syntax highlighting and formatting.
byOptionId.browseronly: Incorrect — a browser displays HTML but doesn't let you author files effectively.
byOptionId.spreadsheet: Incorrect — spreadsheets are not intended for coding HTML.
byOptionId.imageeditor: Incorrect — image editors edit images, not HTML source.

questions.prompt: What file extension should your HTML file have?

byOptionId..html: Correct — .html is the standard extension for HTML documents.
byOptionId..ht: Incorrect — .ht is not related to HTML at all.
byOptionId..docx: Incorrect — .docx is a Word document format, not HTML source.
byOptionId..exe: Incorrect — .exe is an executable file format, not a source file.

questions.prompt: How do you check your HTML in a browser?

options.label: Only by uploading to a server
Open the saved .html file in a browser

explanations.overview: Open the file locally in a browser to preview changes during development.

byOptionId.open-file: Correct — opening the saved .html file in a browser shows the rendered page.
byOptionId.upload: Incorrect — uploading is optional; you can preview files locally.
byOptionId.print: Incorrect — printing doesn't render interactive HTML in a browser.
byOptionId.compile: Incorrect — HTML is not compiled into executables.

questions.prompt: What is the fastest way to see changes in your HTML file?

options.label: Live preview or auto-reload in the editor
Pray to the Whopper Gods to see changes faster
Commit and push to GitHub every hour to see changes on GitHub Pages

explanations.overview: Live preview saves time by automatically refreshing the browser when you save the file.

byOptionId.livepreview: Correct — live preview or auto-reload shows changes instantly.
byOptionId.manual-refresh: Incorrect — manual refresh works, but live preview is faster.
byOptionId.githubpages: Incorrect — GitHub Pages is not a fast preview method.
byOptionId.restartpc: Incorrect — restarting is unnecessary for previewing changes.
